author: gfenno
text: IoT update_thing attributePayload does not take a 'merge' key in Python 2.7.10

author: gfenno
text:
When attempting to perform an update\_thing operation using Python 2.7.10 the response is a ParamValidationError while the same operation succeeds in Python 3.5.2

[Link in Boto3 Docs](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/iot.html#IoT.Client.update_thing)

Boto3 version 1.4.0

Python 2.7.10

```
iot.update_thing(thingName="AttributeTestThing",attributePayload={'merge':True,'attributes':{'test-attribute2':'98'}})
```

* Response

```
botocore.exceptions.ParamValidationError: Parameter validation failed:
Unknown parameter in attributePayload: "merge", must be one of: attributes
```

Python 3.5.2

```
iot.update_thing(thingName="AttributeTestThing",attributePayload={'merge':True,'attributes':{'test-attribute2':'98'}})
```

* Response  
  (proper insertion)

I would just use Python 3 but I need this operation in a lambda function which requires Python 2.7

author: JordonPhillips
text: You will need to update your botocore version for python 2.7 to get that additional parameter.
